questions.prompt: Identify the cleft type: "It was the funding gap that ultimately delayed the project."
questions.explanation: An It-cleft has the structure It + be + [focused element] + that/who + rest of clause. Here: It was + the funding gap (focus) + that + ultimately delayed the project. A Wh-cleft would begin with What…. An All-cleft would begin with All…. A reverse Wh-cleft would put the focused element first followed by is/was + what-clause.

questions.prompt: Complete the Wh-cleft: "___ the delegates achieved was a preliminary framework, not a binding treaty."
questions.explanation: A Wh-cleft begins with a wh-clause as subject: What the delegates achieved was a preliminary framework. "That" would form a different clause type, not a Wh-cleft. "It" starts an It-cleft, which requires a following that/who. "All what" is never correct — the correct limiting cleft uses simply "All" without "what".

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses a cleft to focus on the PLACE.

options.text: What the treaty was signed was in Geneva.
All they signed was in Geneva.
It was in Geneva that the treaty was signed.
Geneva is what signed the treaty.

questions.explanation: To focus on the place, use an It-cleft with the prepositional phrase as the focused element: It was in Geneva that the treaty was signed. The preposition in is retained with the place name. Option (a) forms a Wh-cleft but the word order is wrong (what + place is unusual). Option (b) uses All-cleft inappropriately — it focuses on action, not location. Option (d) inverts meaning (Geneva signed the treaty) and is not a cleft.

options.text: What she objected to was the lack of transparency.
It was because of poor planning that the launch failed.
It is the budget that needs reviewing.
What I did was to consider all the options.

questions.explanation: Option (d) is wrong: after "All/What [subject] did was", the verb takes the bare infinitive, not the to-infinitive. Correct: What I did was consider all the options. The use of "to consider" (to-infinitive) after "did was" is the classic learner error for this cleft type. Options (a), (b), and (c) are all correctly formed — a Wh-cleft, an It-cleft with a because-clause, and an It-cleft in the present tense.

questions.prompt: Rewrite using an All-cleft to capture "She didn't argue or protest — she simply resigned." Which version is correct?

options.text: All what she did was resigning.
All she did was to resign.

questions.explanation: The All-cleft structure is: All + subject + did + was + bare infinitive. So: All she did was resign. "All what she did" inserts an incorrect "what" — it should be just "All". "To resign" (to-infinitive) after "did was" is the common error — the bare infinitive is required. "All did she was" wrongly applies inversion inside the cleft — no inversion occurs in an All-cleft.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal academic sentence to identify the cause: "Many observers blamed mismanagement. However, ___ that derailed the project was the sudden withdrawal of government funding."
questions.explanation: The sentence is a Wh-cleft: What + [relative clause] + was + [focused element]: What derailed the project was the sudden withdrawal of government funding. Only "what" correctly heads this structure. "It was what" would create a double-frame error (It-cleft + Wh-cleft merged). "All what" is never standard. "It" would require the It-cleft structure with that/who: It was [element] that derailed the project — but the sentence as given continues with "that derailed", so the slot before "that" needs the focused element, not just "it".
